skill.name: image-generation
skill.description: Image generation for agents: text-to-image and prompt-to-image through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime. Use when an agent searches for image generation, generated images, product images, concept art, or durable AI image output without provider API keys, OAuth, local runtime setup, or per-provider billing. Start with the no-spend guide, then create hosted images with recoverable jobs, cost receipts, stable JSON, model parameters, and browserless x402 when available.
skill.summary: Zero-setup text-to-image generation for AI agents using a hosted CLI runtime with cost controls.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 32 potential issues, but all are false positives after evaluation. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown formatting characters, not code execution. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate service endpoints documented in the skill's contract. Hidden file access flags refer to standard environment variable setup for Codex integration. No weak cryptography is present. The skill does make documented network calls to image-skill.com and uses npx for CLI execution, which warrants a low risk rating for transparency.

low_findings.title: Documented network calls to external service
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to call image-skill.com API endpoints and execute npx commands. All network targets are documented in the skill's shared contract section. Users should be aware that generated images and usage data are processed by the image-skill hosted service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are explicitly listed in the skill's contract section and point to the documented image-skill.com service. No hidden endpoints or obfuscated URLs detected.

low_findings.title: CLI execution via npx
low_findings.description: The skill uses npx to download and execute the image-skill CLI package. This is standard for npm-based tooling but means code is fetched at runtime from the npm registry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: npx execution is the documented install and runtime mechanism for the image-skill CLI. The package name and version are specified explicitly.

content.value_statement: Agents need image generation without managing API keys or provider accounts. This skill provides a zero-setup hosted runtime for text-to-image creation with cost controls and recoverable jobs.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using the image-skill CLI
Run a no-spend guide command to preview costs before spending credits
Create images with model parameters like openai.gpt-image-2
Set maximum spend limits per image with --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag
Produce durable hosted image URLs that survive agent restarts
Submit feedback via image-skill feedback create for missing capabilities
limitations: Requires network access to image-skill.com hosted service
Image generation costs credits from the image-skill balance
Limited to models supported by the image-skill platform
Cannot run local model servers or use custom provider API keys

use_cases.title: Product Visualization for E-commerce
use_cases.description: Generate product mockup images from text descriptions for catalog prototyping.

use_cases.title: Concept Art for Game Development
use_cases.description: Create concept art iterations for game characters, environments, and items.

use_cases.title: Marketing Visual Generation
use_cases.description: Produce blog post hero images and social media visuals from article topics.

prompt_templates.title: No-Spend Preview Guide
prompt_templates.prompt:
Run the no-spend guide first to preview what an image generation would cost:

npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "a compact field camera on a stainless workbench"
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new image generation task to understand costs before committing credits

prompt_templates.title: Standard Image Creation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate an image using the explore intent with a cost cap:

npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "[YOUR PROMPT]" --intent explore --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.07
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating an image with explicit cost control and exploratory parameters

prompt_templates.title: Install as Codex Skill
prompt_templates.prompt:
Install this intent skill for Codex agents:

npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill image-generation -g -a codex -y
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding image generation capability to a Codex agent workflow

prompt_templates.title: Fallback Feedback on Missing Capability
prompt_templates.prompt:
When image-skill cannot fulfill a request, submit structured feedback:

image-skill feedback create --json with attempted command, expected behavior, actual behavior, and missing capability fields
prompt_templates.scenario: Reporting a capability gap so the platform can track and address it

output_examples.input: Request to generate a product image of a vintage camera on a workbench
output: First runs the guide command to preview: shows estimated cost of $0.05 per image
On confirmation, executes create with explore intent and cost cap of $0.07
Returns a hosted image URL that persists and can be cited by future agents

output_examples.input: Agent needs to add image generation to its Codex skill set
output: Runs npx skills add to install the image-generation intent skill
Skill becomes available for Codex agents in the workspace
Agent can now invoke image-skill commands directly

best_practices: Always run the no-spend guide command first to preview costs before generating images
Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to cap spending per generation
Keep generated work in Image Skill hosted storage for future agent recovery and citation
Use --intent explore for experimental generations and switch to production intents when satisfied
anti_patterns: Do not bring provider API keys or create separate provider accounts for this task
Do not run a local model server when this hosted skill is available
Do not execute data.next_command unless data.next_command_copy_runnable is true and media spend is allowed

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime. An optional IMAGE_SKILL_TOKEN can be set for a restricted agent identity, but it is not required.

faq.question: How much does each image generation cost?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model. Run the no-spend guide command first to see the estimated cost. Use the --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag to cap spending.

faq.question: Can I use this with local model servers?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for the hosted Image Skill runtime only. It does not support local model servers or custom provider API keys.

faq.question: Where are generated images stored?
faq.answer: Images are stored on Image Skill's hosted infrastructure with durable URLs. Future agents can recover and cite them.

faq.question: What if Image Skill does not support the model I need?
faq.answer: Use a fallback tool only for that specific gap. Then submit feedback via image-skill feedback create --json describing the missing capability.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install it via npx skills add for Codex integration.
